page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2.xlarge is an x86_64 architecture instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 8 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing a total of 8 vCPUs. The memory configuration consists of 32.0 GB of RAM, equating to 4.0 GB per core. This instance operates on the KVM hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is limited to a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the ecs.sn2.xlarge generally delivers lower-tier performance. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests via stress-ng place the instance in the bottom 25%. Similarly, memory bandwidth read operations and Passmark memory scores fall into the weak category, though large-block memory write bandwidth achieves an average rating. Computational tasks such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression register poor performance, landing in the bottom 10%. For lightweight workloads, the instance achieves average performance in small-model LLM inference tasks, but struggles with larger models.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the ecs.sn2.xlarge offers a stable resource profile for general-purpose workloads that do not require high-performance compute or local storage. Its qualitative cost efficiency is aligned with legacy hardware tiers, making it suitable for moderate, non-latency-sensitive applications. Appropriate workloads include basic web serving, lightweight background processing, small-scale database operations, and development environments that benefit from dedicated CPU cores but do not demand high-throughput I/O or advanced vector processing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2.xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8269CY vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates that the instance generally delivers lower-tier performance, with stress-ng CPU tests and memory read bandwidth falling into the bottom 25%, while large-block memory write bandwidth and small-model LLM inference achieve average ratings. Given its dedicated CPU allocation and balanced 1:4 memory-to-vCPU ratio, the ecs.sn2.xlarge is qualitatively cost-effective for stable, non-intensive workloads. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, moderate web serving, development environments, and lightweight background processing tasks that do not require high-throughput local storage or intensive computational power.
